Home / Alternatives to Google Forms / Google Forms vs LimeSurvey

Google Forms vs LimeSurvey

A side-by-side look at Google Forms (the paid SaaS) and LimeSurvey (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Google Forms LimeSurvey
Tagline Free forms attached to Google Sheets. Long-established open source survey platform.
License Proprietary SaaS GPL-2.0
Pricing Free. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 3/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Any survey where Google Forms' logic runs out of gas.

LimeSurvey strengths

  • Deep question types and logic.
  • Used widely in research and academia.
  • Self-hosted or paid cloud.

LimeSurvey weaknesses

  • UI feels dated.
  • Setup wizard is clunky.
  • Theming requires effort to match modern brands.

What's the catch with Google Forms?

  • Basic styling options.
  • Data lives in Google cloud.
  • Limited logic branching.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Google Forms: see Google Forms alternatives, or learn more about LimeSurvey on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
